completed_tasks.note: Cycle 27: Vercel deployment configuration. Created apps/web/vercel.json: Next.js framework declaration, Turborepo-aware buildCommand (pnpm turbo run build --filter=@worldpulse/web), pnpm installCommand, 30s function maxDuration for API routes, Sentry monitoring-tunnel rewrite (matches tunnelRoute in next.config.mjs), Cache-Control no-store for /api/ routes, ignoreCommand (git diff monorepo-aware to skip redundant builds), env block documenting NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL/WS_URL/APP_URL. Fixed next.config.mjs CSP connect-src: localhost:3001 and ws://localhost:3001 now conditionally included only when NODE_ENV !== 'production' — production CSP no longer leaks localhost origin. Created docs/deployment/VERCEL.md: full deployment guide covering monorepo Root Directory setting, all required env vars (runtime/Sentry/Auth), CSP behavior explanation, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL fallback pattern documentation, preview URL CORS setup, manual deploy CLI commands, troubleshooting table. Git commit pending (NTFS index.lock — same Windows cross-platform issue as cycles 22-26). Pending commit command: git add apps/web/vercel.json apps/web/next.config.mjs docs/deployment/VERCEL.md && git commit -m 'feat(vercel): add vercel.json config, fix prod CSP localhost leak, add VERCEL.md deployment guide'. Next: Integration — Cloudflare Security Headers & CDN Preparation (Task 23, P11).

completed_tasks.phase: Integration — Sentry Error Tracking & Performance Monitoring
completed_tasks.note: Cycle 26: Sentry integration fully implemented. Added @sentry/nextjs ^8.0.0 to apps/web/package.json and @sentry/node ^8.0.0 to apps/api/package.json. Created apps/web/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx — React class error boundary that calls Sentry.captureException() and shows friendly fallback UI. Wired ErrorBoundary into apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx wrapping children. Updated apps/web/next.config.mjs with withSentryConfig() wrapper (source map upload, tree shaking, tunnel route). Created docs/deployment/SENTRY.md documenting required env vars (NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN, SENTRY_DSN, SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN, SENTRY_ORG, SENTRY_PROJECT). Existing sentry.client/server/edge.config.ts and apps/api/src/lib/sentry.ts (initSentry, captureException, flushSentry) were already complete — only the package installs and missing pieces added. Git commit pending (NTFS index.lock from Windows process — known issue). Next: Integration — Vercel Deployment Configuration (Task 22, P11).

completed_tasks.phase: Gate 1 — Scraper Stability Validation & 14-Day Health Monitor
completed_tasks.note: Cycle 25: Gate 1 audit + validation complete. Audit confirmed 30 OSINT pollers + 1 RSS poller (54 total feeds) all wrapped in try/catch with circuit breakers. Pre-existing stability-tracker.ts had full Redis key implementation (scraper:stability:consecutive_clean_hours, last_failure_at, status), evaluateCleanHour() with 70% threshold, runStabilityCheck() with streak/reset logic and [STABILITY] log lines, 60-min cron in scraper/src/index.ts, and GET /api/v1/admin/scraper/stability endpoint (5-min Redis cache, all required response fields). Added stability.test.ts (21 tests): clean hour detection, streak increment/reset, percent_to_gate at 0/50/100%, estimated_gate_clear_date, status:'stable' only at streak≥336, API response shape, last_failure_at null handling, failureReason formatting. 14-day stability clock can now start — infrastructure is complete and verified.

Cycle 24: Cyber Threat Intelligence page. Backend: cyber.ts (253L) — GET /api/v1/cyber/recent (query signals WHERE category='security' or source_slug IN cisa-kev/otx-threats, time window 24h/48h/7d, Redis cache 3-min, 60rpm rate limit) + GET /api/v1/cyber/summary (total_24h, cisa_kev_count, otx_count, critical/high/medium/low breakdown, 3-min cache). Frontend: cyber-threats/page.tsx (419L) — 4 KPI cards (total threats, CISA KEV, OTX Pulses, Critical count), threat feed with severity color-coding (critical=red, high=orange, medium=yellow, low=gray), CISA KEV badge (blue) + OTX Pulse badge (purple), time window selector 24h/48h/7d, 5-min auto-refresh. LeftSidebar updated with cyber-threats link (🔒, red NEW badge). cyber.test.ts (264L, 10+ test cases). registerCyberRoutes registered in index.ts. Git commit pending (NTFS index.lock from Windows/Linux cross-platform issue — same as cycles 12, 18, 19, 23). Unique: no competitor (Ground News, GDELT, Reuters Connect, AP Wire, OpenClaw, Crucix) has a Cyber Threat Intelligence page in a consumer news intelligence platform. Counters GDELT's newly enhanced Cyber Events Database (detected cycle 24 competition scan). Completes WorldPulse unique intelligence layer stack: space weather + internet outages + sanctions + finance + cyber threats.

completed_tasks.phase: Competitive — GDELT Summary Visual News Imagery on Signal Detail Pages
completed_tasks.note: Cycle 23: GDELT Summary Visual News Imagery. GET /api/v1/signals/:id/news-images endpoint added to signals.ts: reuses extractTVKeywords() for keyword extraction; queries GDELT DOC API mode=artlist (returns article socialimage Open Graph images); filters out articles with empty socialimage; AbortSignal.timeout(5000) on fetch — all errors return empty images array, never 500; Redis cache key news-images:{signalId} TTL 3600s (1 hour); rate limited 30 req/min. NewsImages.tsx (240L): 3-col grid (lg), 2-col (sm), 1-col (mobile); lazy-loaded img thumbnails with onError fallback; domain badge + caption + date; external link overlay on hover; View Article CTA (purple); hidden when 0 images; GDELT Summary attribution. Wired into SignalDetailClient.tsx after <TVClips> (import + JSX). news-images.test.ts (220L, 10 test cases): constants, extractTVKeywords, success path, socialimage filter, network error, AbortError timeout, Redis cache hit, 404 signal not found, cache TTL assertion, NewsImage shape validation. TypeScript strict: 0 errors. Directly counters GDELT Summary standalone product. Signal enrichment stack after cycle 23: reliability + virality + verification timeline + YouTube/podcast + TV clips (unique) + visual news imagery (unique).

completed_tasks.phase: Competitive — GDELT TV News Clip Integration on Signal Detail Pages
completed_tasks.note: Cycle 22: GDELT TV News Clip Integration. GET /api/v1/signals/:id/tv-clips endpoint added to signals.ts: extractTVKeywords() strips stop words, returns up to 3 keywords; recent signals (≤7d) use timespan=7d, older signals use startdatetime/enddatetime brackets; AbortSignal.timeout(5000) on GDELT fetch — all errors return empty clips array, never 500; Redis cache key tv-clips:{signalId} TTL 1800s; rate limited 30 req/min. TVClips.tsx (227L): 3-card skeleton loader, 2-col grid, station badge + show name + relative date + duration + thumbnail with play-overlay hover + Watch Clip CTA + GDELT TV attribution; hidden when 0 clips. Wired into SignalDetailClient.tsx after <SignalMedia>. tv-clips.test.ts (390L, 10 test cases): extractTVKeywords, formatGdeltDate, route success/error/timeout/cache-hit/404/old-signal/recent-signal/rate-limit. Directly counters GDELT's March 19, 2026 TV News Visual Explorer. No competitor (Ground News, Reuters Connect, AP Wire) surfaces TV news clips on intelligence signal pages.
